{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks whether global management at the same centre improves outcomes for patients with colorectal cancer and synchronous liver metastases. The retrieved documents from MEDCORP emphasize the importance of an interprofessional approach involving medical, radiologic, and surgical oncology teams. Document [27] specifically states that interprofessional team communication and proper care coordination are paramount, and that patients should receive treatment within a larger hospital system with higher patient volumes and open access to patient information, so all team members operate from the same up-to-date data for better care. This supports the idea that centralized, coordinated management improves outcomes. Document [18] and [19] also highlight the necessity of interdisciplinary evaluation to discuss optimal treatment plans, including perioperative chemotherapy, which is critical for curative intent. The consistent theme across multiple documents is that coordinated, multidisciplinary care at a single centre leads to better patient outcomes through improved communication, shared data, and unified treatment planning. Therefore, the evidence strongly supports that global management at the same centre improves results.", "answer_choice": "A"}
